body_txt: Not all prisons are made with walls, locks, and bars. Sometimes, other forces, usually of the supernatural kind, such as a Curse , keep a certain character restrained to a specific area. They can freely move within the confines of this area, but any attempt by them to leave is somehow doomed to fail. The reason why can vary. Perhaps there is Some Kind of Force Field around the area that keeps them inside. Or the place they reside in is essential to their existence somehow, and leaving would instantly kill them . And the worst part is, it only affects them; everybody else can come and go as they please, unhindered by the forces that keep the bound character trapped, further underscoring that these characters are effectively prisoners despite not actually being locked up. Closely related to Closed Circle , but while that trope deals with circumstances that prevent everybody from entering or leaving a place for the time being, often for plot-related reasons such as making sure characters are on their own and can’t bring in outside help, this trope is about only a few characters being restricted to an area while everyone else is not. If the trapped character is a main character, or at least the central focus of the current story, expect the plot to revolve around finding a way to free this character from their bonds. For enemies of the character, the fact that their opponent is bound to an area provides them with an easy Logical Weakness they can exploit; if they can outrun the character and cross the border of the area, their opponent will be forced to give up the chase. For supernatural beings, these areas may be at a Thin Dimensional Barrier or Place of Power . The area in question could be a Tailor-Made Prison . See also Haunted Fetter for spirits that are anchored to some kind of object. Compare Unnaturally Looping Location , where the character is literally restricted to a single, infinitely looping location (such as a single hallway), usually temporarily.
